   524→#[cfg(test)]
   525→#[allow(clippy::unwrap_used)]
   526→mod tests {
   527→    use super::*;
   528→
   529→    // ── v3/v4 connect params round-trip ────────────────────────────────
   530→
   531→    #[test]
   532→    fn v3_connect_params_round_trip() {
   533→        let json = serde_json::json!({
   534→            "minProtocol": 3,
   535→            "maxProtocol": 3,
   536→            "client": { "id": "test", "version": "0.1.0", "platform": "browser", "mode": "operator" },
   537→            "locale": "en",
   538→        });
   539→        let params: ConnectParams = serde_json::from_value(json).unwrap();
   540→        assert_eq!(params.min_protocol, 3);
   541→        assert_eq!(params.max_protocol, 3);
   542→        assert_eq!(params.client.id, "test");
   543→        assert_eq!(params.locale.as_deref(), Some("en"));
   544→    }
   545→
   546→    #[test]
   547→    fn v4_connect_params_parses_and_converts() {
   548→        let json = serde_json::json!({
   549→            "protocol": { "min": 3, "max": 4 },
   550→            "client": { "id": "test-v4", "version": "0.2.0", "platform": "browser", "mode": "operator" },
   551→            "locale": "fr",
   552→            "extensions": {
   553→                "moltis": {
   554→                    "caps": ["audio"],
   555→                    "pathEnv": "/usr/bin"
   556→                }
   557→            }
   558→        });
   559→        let v4: ConnectParamsV4 = serde_json::from_value(json).unwrap();
   560→        assert_eq!(v4.protocol.min, 3);
   561→        assert_eq!(v4.protocol.max, 4);
   562→
   563→        let params = v4.into_connect_params();
   564→        assert_eq!(params.min_protocol, 3);
   565→        assert_eq!(params.max_protocol, 4);
   566→        assert_eq!(params.client.id, "test-v4");
   567→        assert_eq!(params.caps.as_ref().unwrap(), &["audio"]);
   568→        assert_eq!(params.path_env.as_deref(), Some("/usr/bin"));
   569→    }
   570→
   571→    #[test]
   572→    fn v4_connect_params_empty_extensions() {
   573→        let json = serde_json::json!({
   574→            "protocol": { "min": 4, "max": 4 },
   575→            "client": { "id": "minimal", "version": "1.0", "platform": "cli", "mode": "operator" },
   576→        });
   577→        let v4: ConnectParamsV4 = serde_json::from_value(json).unwrap();
   578→        let params = v4.into_connect_params();
   579→        assert!(params.caps.is_none());
   580→        assert!(params.device.is_none());
   581→        assert!(params.path_env.is_none());
   582→    }

   586→    #[test]

   588→        let frame = EventFrame::new("chat", serde_json::json!({"text":"hi"}), 1);
   589→        let json = serde_json::to_value(&frame).unwrap();
   590→        assert!(!json.as_object().unwrap().contains_key("stream"));

   713→    // ── Error codes ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   714→
   715→    #[test]
   716→    fn new_error_codes_exist() {
   717→        assert_eq!(error_codes::UNKNOWN_METHOD, "UNKNOWN_METHOD");
   718→        assert_eq!(error_codes::UNAUTHORIZED, "UNAUTHORIZED");
   719→        assert_eq!(error_codes::FORBIDDEN, "FORBIDDEN");
   720→        assert_eq!(error_codes::NOT_FOUND, "NOT_FOUND");
   721→        assert_eq!(error_codes::CONFLICT, "CONFLICT");
   722→        assert_eq!(error_codes::RATE_LIMITED, "RATE_LIMITED");
   723→        assert_eq!(error_codes::TIMEOUT, "TIMEOUT");

1→//! Gateway WebSocket/RPC protocol definitions.
     2→//!
     3→//! Protocol version 4 (backward-compatible with v3). All communication uses JSON frames over WebSocket.
     4→//!
     5→//! Frame types:
     6→//! - `RequestFrame`  — client → gateway RPC call (also server → client in v4)
     7→//! - `ResponseFrame` — gateway → client RPC result (also client → server in v4)
     8→//! - `EventFrame`    — gateway → client server-push
     9→
    10→use std::collections::HashMap;
    11→
    12→use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
    13→
    14→// ── Constants ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    15→
    16→pub const PROTOCOL_VERSION: u32 = 4;
    17→pub const MAX_PAYLOAD_BYTES: usize = 524_288; // 512 KB
    18→pub const MAX_BUFFERED_BYTES: usize = 1_572_864; // 1.5 MB
    19→pub const TICK_INTERVAL_MS: u64 = 30_000; // 30s
    20→pub const HANDSHAKE_TIMEOUT_MS: u64 = 10_000; // 10s
    21→pub const DEDUPE_TTL_MS: u64 = 300_000; // 5 min
    22→pub const DEDUPE_MAX_ENTRIES: usize = 1_000;
    23→
    24→// ── Subscriptions ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    25→
    26→pub mod subscriptions {
    27→    /// Wildcard subscription: receive all events.
    28→    pub const WILDCARD: &str = "*";
    29→}
    30→
    31→// ── Error codes ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    32→
    33→pub mod error_codes {
    34→    // v3 backward-compat codes
    35→    pub const NOT_LINKED: &str = "NOT_LINKED";
    36→    pub const NOT_PAIRED: &str = "NOT_PAIRED";
    37→    pub const AGENT_TIMEOUT: &str = "AGENT_TIMEOUT";
    38→    pub const INVALID_REQUEST: &str = "INVALID_REQUEST";
    39→    pub const UNAVAILABLE: &str = "UNAVAILABLE";
    40→
    41→    // v4 standardized codes
    42→    pub const UNKNOWN_METHOD: &str = "UNKNOWN_METHOD";
    43→    pub const UNAUTHORIZED: &str = "UNAUTHORIZED";
    44→    pub const FORBIDDEN: &str = "FORBIDDEN";
    45→    pub const NOT_FOUND: &str = "NOT_FOUND";
    46→    pub const CONFLICT: &str = "CONFLICT";
    47→    pub const RATE_LIMITED: &str = "RATE_LIMITED";
    48→    pub const TIMEOUT: &str = "TIMEOUT";
    49→    pub const INTERNAL: &str = "INTERNAL";
    50→    pub const PROTOCOL_ERROR: &str = "PROTOCOL_ERROR";
    51→    pub const PAYLOAD_TOO_LARGE: &str = "PAYLOAD_TOO_LARGE";
    52→}
    53→
    54→// ── Error shape ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    55→
    56→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
    57→pub struct ErrorShape {
    58→    pub code: String,
    59→    pub message: String,
    60→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    61→    pub details: Option<serde_json::Value>,
    62→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    63→    pub retryable: Option<bool>,
    64→    #[serde(rename = "retryAfterMs", skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    65→    pub retry_after_ms: Option<u64>,
    66→}
    67→
    68→impl ErrorShape {
    69→    pub fn new(code: impl Into<String>, message: impl Into<String>) -> Self {
    70→        Self {
    71→            code: code.into(),
    72→            message: message.into(),
    73→            details: None,
    74→            retryable: None,
    75→            retry_after_ms: None,
    76→        }
    77→    }
    78→}
    79→
    80→// ── Frames ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    81→
    82→/// Client → gateway RPC request. Also used server → client in v4 bidirectional RPC.
    83→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
    84→pub struct RequestFrame {
    85→    pub r#type: String, // always "req"
    86→    pub id: String,
    87→    pub method: String,
    88→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    89→    pub params: Option<serde_json::Value>,
    90→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
    91→    pub channel: Option<String>,
    92→}
    93→
    94→/// Gateway → client RPC response. Also client → server in v4 bidirectional RPC.
    95→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
    96→pub struct ResponseFrame {
    97→    pub r#type: String, // always "res"
    98→    pub id: String,
    99→    pub ok: bool,
   100→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   101→    pub payload: Option<serde_json::Value>,
   102→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   103→    pub error: Option<ErrorShape>,
   104→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   105→    pub channel: Option<String>,
   106→}
   107→
   108→impl ResponseFrame {
   109→    pub fn ok(id: impl Into<String>, payload: serde_json::Value) -> Self {
   110→        Self {
   111→            r#type: "res".into(),
   112→            id: id.into(),
   113→            ok: true,
   114→            payload: Some(payload),
   115→            error: None,
   116→            channel: None,
   117→        }
   118→    }
   119→
   120→    pub fn err(id: impl Into<String>, error: ErrorShape) -> Self {
   121→        Self {
   122→            r#type: "res".into(),
   123→            id: id.into(),
   124→            ok: false,
   125→            payload: None,
   126→            error: Some(error),
   127→            channel: None,
   128→        }
   129→    }
   130→}
   131→
   132→/// Gateway → client server-push event.
   133→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
   134→pub struct EventFrame {
   135→    pub r#type: String, // always "event"
   136→    pub event: String,
   137→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   138→    pub payload: Option<serde_json::Value>,
   139→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   140→    pub seq: Option<u64>,
   141→    #[serde(rename = "stateVersion", skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   142→    pub state_version: Option<StateVersion>,
   143→    /// Stream group ID for chunked delivery (v4).
   144→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   145→    pub stream: Option<String>,
   146→    /// End-of-stream marker (v4).
   147→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   148→    pub done: Option<bool>,
   149→    /// Logical channel for multiplexing (v4).
   150→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   151→    pub channel: Option<String>,
   152→}
   153→
   154→impl EventFrame {
   155→    pub fn new(event: impl Into<String>, payload: serde_json::Value, seq: u64) -> Self {
   156→        Self {
   157→            r#type: "event".into(),
   158→            event: event.into(),
   159→            payload: Some(payload),
   160→            seq: Some(seq),
   161→            state_version: None,
   162→            stream: None,
   163→            done: None,
   164→            channel: None,
   165→        }
   166→    }
   167→
   168→    /// Create an event frame with stream metadata.
   169→    pub fn streamed(
   170→        event: impl Into<String>,
   171→        payload: serde_json::Value,
   172→        seq: u64,
   173→        stream_id: String,
   174→        done: bool,
   175→    ) -> Self {
   176→        Self {
   177→            r#type: "event".into(),
   178→            event: event.into(),
   179→            payload: Some(payload),
   180→            seq: Some(seq),
   181→            state_version: None,
   182→            stream: Some(stream_id),
   183→            done: Some(done),
   184→            channel: None,
   185→        }
   186→    }
   187→}
   188→
   189→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
   190→pub struct StateVersion {
   191→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   192→    pub presence: Option<u64>,
   193→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   194→    pub health: Option<u64>,
   195→}
   196→
   197→/// Discriminated union of all frame types.
   198→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
   199→#[serde(tag = "type")]
   200→pub enum GatewayFrame {
   201→    #[serde(rename = "req")]
   202→    Request(RequestFrameInner),
   203→    #[serde(rename = "res")]
   204→    Response(ResponseFrameInner),
   205→    #[serde(rename = "event")]
   206→    Event(EventFrameInner),
   207→}
   208→
   209→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
   210→pub struct RequestFrameInner {
   211→    pub id: String,
   212→    pub method: String,
   213→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   214→    pub params: Option<serde_json::Value>,
   215→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   216→    pub channel: Option<String>,
   217→}
   218→
   219→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
   220→pub struct ResponseFrameInner {
   221→    pub id: String,
   222→    pub ok: bool,
   223→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   224→    pub payload: Option<serde_json::Value>,
   225→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   226→    pub error: Option<ErrorShape>,
   227→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   228→    pub channel: Option<String>,
   229→}
   230→
   231→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
   232→pub struct EventFrameInner {
   233→    pub event: String,
   234→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   235→    pub payload: Option<serde_json::Value>,
   236→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   237→    pub seq: Option<u64>,
   238→    #[serde(rename = "stateVersion", skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   239→    pub state_version: Option<StateVersion>,
   240→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   241→    pub stream: Option<String>,
   242→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   243→    pub done: Option<bool>,
   244→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   245→    pub channel: Option<String>,
   246→}
   247→
   248→// ── Extensions ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   249→
   250→/// Namespaced extension data for protocol-agnostic transport.
   251→pub type Extensions = HashMap<String, serde_json::Value>;
   252→
   253→// ── Connect handshake ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   254→
   255→/// Protocol version range for v4 negotiation.
   256→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
   257→pub struct ProtocolRange {
   258→    pub min: u32,
   259→    pub max: u32,
   260→}
   261→
   262→/// v4 connect parameters: flat core + namespaced extensions.
   263→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
   264→pub struct ConnectParamsV4 {
   265→    pub protocol: ProtocolRange,
   266→    pub client: ClientInfo,
   267→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   268→    pub role: Option<String>,
   269→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   270→    pub scopes: Option<Vec<String>>,
   271→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   272→    pub auth: Option<ConnectAuth>,
   273→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   274→    pub locale: Option<String>,
   275→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   276→    pub timezone: Option<String>,
   277→    #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "HashMap::is_empty")]
   278→    pub extensions: Extensions,
   279→}
   280→
   281→impl ConnectParamsV4 {
   282→    /// Convert to v3-compatible `ConnectParams` for internal processing.
   283→    pub fn into_connect_params(self) -> ConnectParams {
   284→        // Extract Moltis-specific fields from extensions.
   285→        let moltis = self.extensions.get("moltis");
   286→        let caps = moltis
   287→            .and_then(|v| v.get("caps"))
   288→            .and_then(|v| serde_json::from_value(v.clone()).ok());
   289→        let commands = moltis
   290→            .and_then(|v| v.get("commands"))
   291→            .and_then(|v| serde_json::from_value(v.clone()).ok());
   292→        let permissions = moltis
   293→            .and_then(|v| v.get("permissions"))
   294→            .and_then(|v| v.as_object().cloned());
   295→        let path_env = moltis
   296→            .and_then(|v| v.get("pathEnv"))
   297→            .and_then(|v| v.as_str())
   298→            .map(String::from);
   299→        let device = moltis
   300→            .and_then(|v| v.get("device"))
   301→            .and_then(|v| serde_json::from_value(v.clone()).ok());
   302→        let user_agent = moltis
   303→            .and_then(|v| v.get("userAgent"))
   304→            .and_then(|v| v.as_str())
   305→            .map(String::from);
   306→
   307→        ConnectParams {
   308→            min_protocol: self.protocol.min,
   309→            max_protocol: self.protocol.max,
   310→            client: self.client,
   311→            caps,
   312→            commands,
   313→            permissions,
   314→            path_env,
   315→            role: self.role,
   316→            scopes: self.scopes,
   317→            device,
   318→            auth: self.auth,
   319→            locale: self.locale,
   320→            user_agent,
   321→            timezone: self.timezone,
   322→        }
   323→    }
   324→}
   325→
   326→/// Parameters sent by the client in the initial `connect` request (v3 format).
   327→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
   328→pub struct ConnectParams {
   329→    #[serde(rename = "minProtocol")]
   330→    pub min_protocol: u32,
   331→    #[serde(rename = "maxProtocol")]
   332→    pub max_protocol: u32,
   333→    pub client: ClientInfo,
   334→    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   335→    pub caps: Option<Vec<String>>,

   524→#[cfg(test)]

   526→mod tests {
   527→    use super::*;
   528→
   529→    // ── v3/v4 connect params round-trip ────────────────────────────────
   530→
   531→    #[test]
   532→    fn v3_connect_params_round_trip() {
   533→        let json = serde_json::json!({
   534→            "minProtocol": 3,
   535→            "maxProtocol": 3,
   536→            "client": { "id": "test", "version": "0.1.0", "platform": "browser", "mode": "operator" },
   537→            "locale": "en",
   538→        });
   539→        let params: ConnectParams = serde_json::from_value(json).unwrap();
   540→        assert_eq!(params.min_protocol, 3);
   541→        assert_eq!(params.max_protocol, 3);
   542→        assert_eq!(params.client.id, "test");
   543→        assert_eq!(params.locale.as_deref(), Some("en"));
   544→    }
   545→
   546→    #[test]
   547→    fn v4_connect_params_parses_and_converts() {
   548→        let json = serde_json::json!({
   549→            "protocol": { "min": 3, "max": 4 },
   550→            "client": { "id": "test-v4", "version": "0.2.0", "platform": "browser", "mode": "operator" },
   551→            "locale": "fr",
   552→            "extensions": {
   553→                "moltis": {
   554→                    "caps": ["audio"],
   555→                    "pathEnv": "/usr/bin"
   556→                }
   557→            }
   558→        });
   559→        let v4: ConnectParamsV4 = serde_json::from_value(json).unwrap();
   560→        assert_eq!(v4.protocol.min, 3);
   561→        assert_eq!(v4.protocol.max, 4);
   562→
   563→        let params = v4.into_connect_params();
   564→        assert_eq!(params.min_protocol, 3);
   565→        assert_eq!(params.max_protocol, 4);
   566→        assert_eq!(params.client.id, "test-v4");
   567→        assert_eq!(params.caps.as_ref().unwrap(), &["audio"]);
   568→        assert_eq!(params.path_env.as_deref(), Some("/usr/bin"));
   569→    }
   570→
   571→    #[test]
   572→    fn v4_connect_params_empty_extensions() {
   573→        let json = serde_json::json!({
   574→            "protocol": { "min": 4, "max": 4 },
   575→            "client": { "id": "minimal", "version": "1.0", "platform": "cli", "mode": "operator" },
   576→        });
   577→        let v4: ConnectParamsV4 = serde_json::from_value(json).unwrap();

   583→
   584→    // ── EventFrame with stream/done/channel ────────────────────────────
   585→
   586→    #[test]

   617→        );
   618→        let json = serde_json::to_value(&frame).unwrap();
   619→        assert_eq!(json["done"], true);
   620→    }
   621→
   622→    #[test]
   623→    fn event_frame_round_trip_with_channel() {
   624→        let mut frame = EventFrame::new("chat", serde_json::json!({}), 1);
   625→        frame.channel = Some("session:abc".into());

   630→
   631→    // ── RequestFrame with channel ──────────────────────────────────────
   632→
   633→    #[test]
   634→    fn request_frame_channel_serialization() {
   635→        let frame = RequestFrame {
   636→            r#type: "req".into(),

   642→        let json = serde_json::to_value(&frame).unwrap();
   643→        assert_eq!(json["channel"], "session:xyz");

   645→
   646→    #[test]
   647→    fn request_frame_omits_null_channel() {
   648→        let frame = RequestFrame {
   649→            r#type: "req".into(),

   659→    // ── HelloOk extensions ─────────────────────────────────────────────

   661→    #[test]

   686→    #[test]
   687→    fn hello_ok_with_extensions_serialized() {
   688→        let mut extensions = Extensions::new();

   690→        let hello = HelloOk {
   691→            r#type: "hello-ok".into(),
   692→            protocol: 4,
   693→            server: ServerInfo {
   694→                version: "0.1.0".into(),
   695→                commit: None,
   696→                host: None,
   697→                conn_id: "test".into(),
   698→            },
   699→            features: Features {
   700→                methods: vec![],
   701→                events: vec![],
   702→            },
   703→            snapshot: serde_json::json!({}),
   704→            canvas_host_url: None,
   705→            auth: None,
   706→            policy: Policy::default(),

   709→        let json = serde_json::to_value(&hello).unwrap();

   712→
   713→    // ── Error codes ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   714→
   715→    #[test]
   716→    fn new_error_codes_exist() {
   717→        assert_eq!(error_codes::UNKNOWN_METHOD, "UNKNOWN_METHOD");
   718→        assert_eq!(error_codes::UNAUTHORIZED, "UNAUTHORIZED");
   719→        assert_eq!(error_codes::FORBIDDEN, "FORBIDDEN");
   720→        assert_eq!(error_codes::NOT_FOUND, "NOT_FOUND");
   721→        assert_eq!(error_codes::CONFLICT, "CONFLICT");
   722→        assert_eq!(error_codes::RATE_LIMITED, "RATE_LIMITED");
   723→        assert_eq!(error_codes::TIMEOUT, "TIMEOUT");
   724→        assert_eq!(error_codes::INTERNAL, "INTERNAL");
   725→        assert_eq!(error_codes::PROTOCOL_ERROR, "PROTOCOL_ERROR");
   726→        assert_eq!(error_codes::PAYLOAD_TOO_LARGE, "PAYLOAD_TOO_LARGE");
   727→    }
   728→
   729→    // ── GatewayFrame round-trip with new fields ────────────────────────
   730→
   731→    #[test]
   732→    fn gateway_frame_response_round_trip() {
   733→        let json = r#"{"type":"res","id":"1","ok":true,"payload":{"result":"ok"}}"#;
   734→        let frame: GatewayFrame = serde_json::from_str(json).unwrap();
   735→        match frame {

   758→    // ── Schema descriptors ─────────────────────────────────────────────

- `ServerSan` enum + impl
- `required_dns_san_names()`, `append_system_host_sans()`, `default_required_sans()`, `required_server_sans()`, `normalize_sans()`, `is_default_loopback_ip()`, `der_contains_ascii()`

**`crates/tls/src/server.rs`** — HTTP redirect and TLS server code:

```rust
//! TLS certificate management and HTTPS server support.
//!
//! On first run, generates a local CA and server certificate (mkcert-style)
//! so the gateway can serve HTTPS out of the box. A companion plain-HTTP
//! server on a secondary port serves the CA cert for easy download and
//! redirects everything else to HTTPS.

pub mod error;
mod certs;
mod server;

pub use certs::*;
pub use server::*;
```

Important rules:

- `server.rs` needs `use super::{Context, Result};` similarly, and `use super::certs::{LOCALHOST_DOMAIN, ...}` if it references cert items. Actually check which items server.rs uses from certs.rs — it uses `is_localhost_name` which is defined in server.rs itself, so it should be self-contained. 
- Actually, `server.rs` uses `Result` from the error module. Add `use super::{Context, Result};`
- `certs.rs` uses `Result`, `Context` from the error module. Add `use super::{Context, Result};`
- Preserve ALL code exactly, just move it and fix imports
- Keep tests with the code they test
- Write the files using the Write tool
- Do NOT use the Edit tool for these new files — use Write

1→//! Compaction strategy dispatcher.
     2→//!
     3→//! Routes a session history through the [`CompactionMode`] selected in
     4→//! `chat.compaction`. Each strategy lives in its own submodule and owns
     5→//! its own tests; shared boundary / pruning / tool-pair helpers live in
     6→//! [`shared`].
     7→//!
     8→//! Submodules:
     9→//! - [`deterministic`] — zero-LLM replace-all extraction.
    10→//! - [`recency_preserving`] — zero-LLM head + middle-marker + tail.
    11→//! - [`structured`] — LLM head + structured-summary + tail (feature-gated).
    12→//! - [`llm_replace`] — LLM streaming-summary replace-all (feature-gated).
    13→//! - [`shared`] — boundary computation, pruning, tool-pair repair,
    14→//!   message builders.
    15→//! - `test_support` — `#[cfg(test)]`-only stub provider for the LLM modes.
    16→//!
    17→//! See `docs/src/compaction.md` for the full mode comparison and trade-off
    18→//! guidance, and the rustdoc on [`moltis_config::CompactionMode`] for
    19→//! per-variant semantics.

    21→use {

1→//! Shared helpers for OpenAI-compatible streaming with tools.
     2→//!
     3→//! This module provides reusable functions for parsing OpenAI-style SSE streams
     4→//! that include tool calls. Used by openai.rs, github_copilot.rs, and kimi_code.rs.
     5→
     6→mod schema_normalization;
     7→mod strict_mode;
     8→
     9→#[cfg(test)]
    10→mod tests;
    11→
    12→use std::collections::{HashMap, HashSet};
    13→
    14→use {schema_normalization::sanitize_schema_for_openai_compat, serde::Serialize, tracing::trace};
    15→
    16→use moltis_agents::model::{
    17→    ChatMessage, CompletionResponse, StreamEvent, ToolCall, Usage, UserContent,
    18→    decode_tool_call_arguments,
    19→};
    20→
    21→pub use strict_mode::patch_schema_for_strict_mode;
    22→
    23→// ============================================================================
    24→// OpenAI Tool Schema Types
    25→// ============================================================================
    26→// These types enforce the correct structure for OpenAI-compatible APIs.
    27→// Using typed structs instead of manual JSON prevents missing fields at compile time.
    28→//
    29→// References:
    30→// - Chat Completions: https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/function-calling
    31→// - Responses API: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-foundry/openai/how-to/responses
    32→// ============================================================================
    33→
    34→/// Chat Completions API tool format (nested under "function").
    35→///
    36→/// ```json
    37→/// { "type": "function", "function": { "name": "...", ... } }
    38→/// ```
    39→#[derive(Debug, Serialize)]
    40→pub struct ChatCompletionsTool {
    41→    #[serde(rename = "type")]
    42→    pub tool_type: &'static str,
    43→    pub function: ChatCompletionsFunction,
    44→}
    45→
    46→/// The function definition nested inside ChatCompletionsTool.
    47→#[derive(Debug, Serialize)]
    48→pub struct ChatCompletionsFunction {
    49→    pub name: String,
    50→    pub description: String,
    51→    pub parameters: serde_json::Value,
    52→    pub strict: bool,
    53→}
    54→
    55→/// Responses API tool format (flat, name at top level).
    56→///
    57→/// ```json
    58→/// { "type": "function", "name": "...", "parameters": {...}, "strict": true }
    59→/// ```
    60→#[derive(Debug, Serialize)]
    61→pub struct ResponsesApiTool {
    62→    #[serde(rename = "type")]
    63→    pub tool_type: &'static str,
    64→    pub name: String,
    65→    pub description: String,
    66→    pub parameters: serde_json::Value,
    67→    pub strict: bool,
    68→}
    69→
    70→/// Convert tool schemas to OpenAI Chat Completions function-calling format.
    71→///
    72→/// Uses the nested `function` object format required by Chat Completions API:
    73→/// ```json
    74→/// { "type": "function", "function": { "name": "...", ... } }
    75→/// ```
    76→///
    77→/// When `strict` is `true`, patches schemas for strict mode compliance:
    78→/// - `additionalProperties: false` on all object schemas
    79→/// - All properties included in `required` array
    80→/// - Originally-optional properties made nullable via array-form types
    81→///
    82→/// When `strict` is `false`, schemas are sanitized but not patched for strict
    83→/// mode. This avoids array-form types like `["boolean", "null"]` that
    84→/// non-OpenAI backends (e.g. Google via OpenRouter) reject.
    85→///
    86→/// See: <https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/function-calling>
    87→pub fn to_openai_tools(tools: &[serde_json::Value], strict: bool) -> Vec<serde_json::Value> {
    88→    let result: Vec<serde_json::Value> = tools
    89→        .iter()
    90→        .filter_map(|t| {
    91→            let mut params = t["parameters"].clone();
    92→            sanitize_schema_for_openai_compat(&mut params);
    93→            if strict {
    94→                patch_schema_for_strict_mode(&mut params);
    95→            }
    96→
    97→            let name = t["name"].as_str()?.to_string();
    98→            let description = t["description"].as_str().unwrap_or("").to_string();
    99→
   100→            // Use typed struct to ensure all required fields are present
   101→            let tool = ChatCompletionsTool {
   102→                tool_type: "function",
   103→                function: ChatCompletionsFunction {
   104→                    name: name.clone(),
   105→                    description,
   106→                    parameters: params,
   107→                    strict,
   108→                },
   109→            };
   110→
   111→            trace!(tool_name = %name, strict, "converted tool to Chat Completions format");
   112→
   113→            // Serialize to Value for compatibility with existing API
   114→            serde_json::to_value(tool).ok()
   115→        })
   116→        .collect();
   117→
   118→    trace!(tools_count = result.len(), "to_openai_tools complete");
   119→    result
   120→}
   121→
   122→/// Convert tool schemas to OpenAI Responses API function-calling format.
   123→///
   124→/// Uses the flat format required by the Responses API where `name` is at top level:
   125→/// ```json
   126→/// { "type": "function", "name": "...", "parameters": {...}, "strict": true }
   127→/// ```
   128→///
   129→/// This is the format used by OpenAI Codex and the Responses API.
   130→///
   131→/// Patches schemas for strict mode compliance:
   132→/// - `additionalProperties: false` on all object schemas
   133→/// - All properties included in `required` array
   134→///
   135→/// See: <https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-foundry/openai/how-to/responses>
   136→pub fn to_responses_api_tools(tools: &[serde_json::Value]) -> Vec<serde_json::Value> {
   137→    let result: Vec<serde_json::Value> = tools
   138→        .iter()
   139→        .filter_map(|t| {
   140→            // Clone parameters and patch for strict mode
   141→            let mut params = t["parameters"].clone();
   142→            sanitize_schema_for_openai_compat(&mut params);
   143→            patch_schema_for_strict_mode(&mut params);
   144→
   145→            let name = t["name"].as_str()?.to_string();
   146→            let description = t["description"].as_str().unwrap_or("").to_string();
   147→
   148→            // Use typed struct to ensure all required fields are present
   149→            let tool = ResponsesApiTool {
   150→                tool_type: "function",
   151→                name: name.clone(),
   152→                description,
   153→                parameters: params,
   154→                strict: true,
   155→            };
   156→
   157→            trace!(tool_name = %name, "converted tool to Responses API format");
   158→
   159→            // Serialize to Value for compatibility with existing API
   160→            serde_json::to_value(tool).ok()
   161→        })
   162→        .collect();
   163→
   164→    trace!(
   165→        tools_count = result.len(),
   166→        "to_responses_api_tools complete"
   167→    );
   168→    result
   169→}
   170→
   171→/// Convert typed chat messages to Responses API input items.
   172→///
   173→/// Responses API accepts a heterogeneous input array (messages, tool calls, and
   174→/// tool outputs). This keeps one canonical conversion for providers that use
   175→/// Responses transport (SSE or WebSocket).
   176→#[must_use]
   177→pub fn to_responses_input(messages: &[ChatMessage]) -> Vec<serde_json::Value> {
   178→    messages
   179→        .iter()
   180→        .flat_map(|msg| match msg {
   181→            ChatMessage::System { .. } => {
   182→                // System messages are extracted into `instructions`.
   183→                vec![]
   184→            },
   185→            ChatMessage::User { content, .. } => {
   186→                let content_blocks = match content {
   187→                    UserContent::Text(t) => {
   188→                        vec![serde_json::json!({"type": "input_text", "text": t})]
   189→                    },
   190→                    UserContent::Multimodal(parts) => parts
   191→                        .iter()
   192→                        .map(|p| match p {
   193→                            moltis_agents::model::ContentPart::Text(t) => {
   194→                                serde_json::json!({"type": "input_text", "text": t})
   195→                            },
   196→                            moltis_agents::model::ContentPart::Image { media_type, data } => {
   197→                                let data_uri = format!("data:{media_type};base64,{data}");
   198→                                serde_json::json!({
   199→                                    "type": "input_image",
   200→                                    "image_url": data_uri,

   204→                        .collect(),
   205→                };
   206→                vec![serde_json::json!({
   207→                    "role": "user",
   208→                    "content": content_blocks,
   209→                })]
   210→            },
   211→            ChatMessage::Assistant {
   212→                content,
   213→                tool_calls,
   214→            } => {
   215→                if !tool_calls.is_empty() {
   216→                    let mut items: Vec<serde_json::Value> = tool_calls
   217→                        .iter()
   218→                        .map(|tc| {
   219→                            serde_json::json!({
   220→                                "type": "function_call",
   221→                                "call_id": tc.id,
   222→                                "name": tc.name,
   223→                                "arguments": tc.arguments.to_string(),
   224→                            })
   225→                        })
   226→                        .collect();
   227→                    if let Some(text) = content
   228→                        && !text.is_empty()
   229→                    {
   230→                        items.insert(
   231→                            0,
   232→                            serde_json::json!({
   233→                                "type": "message",
   234→                                "role": "assistant",
   235→                                "content": [{"type": "output_text", "text": text}]
   236→                            }),
   237→                        );
   238→                    }
   239→                    items
   240→                } else {
   241→                    let text = content.as_deref().unwrap_or("");
   242→                    vec![serde_json::json!({
   243→                        "type": "message",
   244→                        "role": "assistant",
   245→                        "content": [{"type": "output_text", "text": text}]
   246→                    })]
   247→                }
   248→            },
   249→            ChatMessage::Tool {
   250→                tool_call_id,
   251→                content,
   252→            } => {
   253→                vec![serde_json::json!({
   254→                    "type": "function_call_output",
   255→                    "call_id": tool_call_id,
   256→                    "output": content,
   257→                })]
   258→            },
   259→        })
   260→        .collect()
   261→}
   262→
   263→/// Parse tool_calls from an OpenAI response message (non-streaming).
   264→pub fn parse_tool_calls(message: &serde_json::Value) -> Vec<ToolCall> {
   265→    message["tool_calls"]
   266→        .as_array()
   267→        .map(|tcs| {
   268→            tcs.iter()
   269→                .filter_map(|tc| {
   270→                    let id = tc["id"].as_str()?.to_string();
   271→                    let name = tc["function"]["name"].as_str()?.to_string();
   272→                    let arguments = decode_tool_call_arguments(tc["function"].get("arguments"));
   273→                    Some(ToolCall {
   274→                        id,
   275→                        name,
   276→                        arguments,
   277→                    })
   278→                })
   279→                .collect()
   280→        })
   281→        .unwrap_or_default()
   282→}
   283→
   284→fn usage_value_at_path(usage: &serde_json::Value, path: &[&str]) -> Option<u64> {
   285→    let mut cursor = usage;
   286→    for key in path {
   287→        cursor = cursor.get(*key)?;
   288→    }
   289→    cursor
   290→        .as_u64()
   291→        .or_else(|| cursor.as_str().and_then(|raw| raw.parse::<u64>().ok()))
   292→}
   293→
   294→fn usage_field_u32(usage: &serde_json::Value, paths: &[&[&str]]) -> u32 {
   295→    paths
   296→        .iter()
   297→        .find_map(|path| usage_value_at_path(usage, path))
   298→        .unwrap_or(0) as u32
   299→}
   300→
   301→fn usage_object_from_payload(payload: &serde_json::Value) -> Option<&serde_json::Value> {
   302→    if let Some(usage) = payload.get("usage").filter(|usage| usage.is_object()) {
   303→        return Some(usage);
   304→    }
   305→
   306→    if let Some(usage) = payload
   307→        .get("choices")
   308→        .and_then(serde_json::Value::as_array)
   309→        .and_then(|choices| choices.first())
   310→        .and_then(|choice| choice.get("usage"))
   311→        .filter(|usage| usage.is_object())
   312→    {
   313→        return Some(usage);
   314→    }
   315→
   316→    if let Some(usage) = payload
   317→        .get("choices")
   318→        .and_then(serde_json::Value::as_array)
   319→        .and_then(|choices| choices.first())
   320→        .and_then(|choice| choice.get("delta"))
   321→        .and_then(|delta| delta.get("usage"))
   322→        .filter(|usage| usage.is_object())
   323→    {
   324→        return Some(usage);
   325→    }
   326→
   327→    if let Some(usage) = payload
   328→        .get("choices")
   329→        .and_then(serde_json::Value::as_array)
   330→        .and_then(|choices| choices.first())
   331→        .and_then(|choice| choice.get("message"))

   344→/// Parse usage payloads from OpenAI-compatible backends.
   345→///
   346→/// Different providers use different field names:
   347→/// - OpenAI-style: `prompt_tokens`, `completion_tokens`
   348→/// - Anthropic/MiniMax-style: `input_tokens`, `output_tokens`
   349→/// - Cache fields may be top-level or nested in `*_tokens_details`.
   350→#[must_use]
   351→pub fn parse_openai_compat_usage(usage: &serde_json::Value) -> Usage {
   352→    Usage {
   353→        input_tokens: usage_field_u32(usage, &[
   354→            &["prompt_tokens"],
   355→            &["promptTokens"],
   356→            &["input_tokens"],
   357→            &["inputTokens"],
   358→        ]),
   359→        output_tokens: usage_field_u32(usage, &[
   360→            &["completion_tokens"],
   361→            &["completionTokens"],
   362→            &["output_tokens"],
   363→            &["outputTokens"],
   364→        ]),
   365→        cache_read_tokens: usage_field_u32(usage, &[
   366→            &["prompt_tokens_details", "cached_tokens"],
   367→            &["promptTokensDetails", "cachedTokens"],
   368→            &["input_tokens_details", "cached_tokens"],

   370→            &["cache_read_input_tokens"],
   371→            &["cacheReadInputTokens"],
   372→            &["input_tokens_details", "cache_read_input_tokens"],
   373→            &["inputTokensDetails", "cacheReadInputTokens"],
   374→        ]),
   375→        cache_write_tokens: usage_field_u32(usage, &[
   376→            &["cache_creation_input_tokens"],
   377→            &["cacheCreationInputTokens"],
   378→            &["input_tokens_details", "cache_creation_input_tokens"],
   379→            &["inputTokensDetails", "cacheCreationInputTokens"],
   380→        ]),
   381→    }
   382→}

   384→/// Parse usage from an OpenAI-compatible payload, checking common nesting variants.
   385→///
   386→/// Providers differ on where they place usage metadata:
   387→/// - top-level `usage`
   388→/// - `choices[0].usage`
   389→/// - `choices[0].delta.usage`
   390→/// - `choices[0].message.usage`
   391→/// - provider extension blocks (for example `x_groq.usage`)
   392→#[must_use]
   393→pub fn parse_openai_compat_usage_from_payload(payload: &serde_json::Value) -> Option<Usage> {
   394→    usage_object_from_payload(payload).map(parse_openai_compat_usage)

Here is a summary of what was done:

**Files modified:**
- `/Users/penso/.superset/worktrees/moltis/functional-caution/crates/providers/src/openai_compat/mod.rs` -- Reduced from 977 lines to 25 lines. Now contains only module declarations (`mod provider`, `mod schema_normalization`, `mod strict_mode`, `mod tests`) and re-exports via `pub use`.

**Files created:**
- `/Users/penso/.superset/worktrees/moltis/functional-caution/crates/providers/src/openai_compat/provider.rs` -- 972 lines containing all the implementation code that was previously in `mod.rs`: tool schema types (`ChatCompletionsTool`, `ChatCompletionsFunction`, `ResponsesApiTool`), tool conversion functions (`to_openai_tools`, `to_responses_api_tools`), message conversion (`to_responses_input`), usage parsing (`parse_openai_compat_usage`, etc.), think-tag processing (`strip_think_tags`, streaming state machine), SSE processing (`process_openai_sse_line`, `process_responses_sse_line`), and stream finalization helpers.

**Key details:**
- All public API symbols are re-exported from `mod.rs`, so downstream callers are unaffected.
- The `sanitize_schema_for_openai_compat` import in `mod.rs` is gated with `#[cfg(test)]` since it's only used by the test module via `super::`.
- Imports in `provider.rs` were cleaned up to use `super::schema_normalization::sanitize_schema_for_openai_compat` and `super::strict_mode::patch_schema_for_strict_mode` instead of bare paths.
- All 12 openai_compat tests pass. `cargo check -p moltis-providers` shows no errors from this module.

1→//! GraphQL output and input types.
     2→//!
     3→//! These types are deserialized from the JSON values returned by service
     4→//! methods. They use `#[derive(SimpleObject)]` for output types and
     5→//! `#[derive(InputObject)]` for input types. Fields use `serde` for
     6→//! deserialization and `async-graphql` for schema generation.
     7→//!
     8→//! For dynamic/untyped fields, the `Json` scalar is used.
     9→
    10→use {async_graphql::SimpleObject, serde::Deserialize};
    11→
    12→use crate::scalars::Json;
    13→
    14→// ── Common result type ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    15→

Working directory: /Users/penso/.superset/worktrees/moltis/functional-caution

Rule: "Do not add implementation code to `mod.rs` or `lib.rs`. Keep those files for module wiring, exports, and crate setup."

Steps:
1. Read `crates/agents/src/runner/mod.rs` fully (read in parts if needed, ~617 lines)
2. It has submodule declarations: `mod non_streaming; pub mod retry; mod streaming; pub mod tool_result; #[cfg(test)] mod tests; #[cfg(test)] mod tests_legacy;` — keep ALL of these in mod.rs
3. It has `pub use` re-exports — keep those in mod.rs
4. Everything else (imports, constants, types like AgentRunError, AgentRunResult, UsageAccumulator, RunnerEvent, OnEvent, and all ~20 utility functions) goes into a new file
5. Create `crates/agents/src/runner/helpers.rs` with ALL the implementation code (constants, types, functions)
6. Update mod.rs to add `mod helpers;` and re-export what's needed. Since many items are used by the sibling submodules (non_streaming, streaming), check what visibility they need. Items that were `pub(super)` or bare `fn` need to be `pub(super)` in helpers.rs so siblings can still access them via `super::helpers::` or through re-exports.

Actually, the simplest approach: move the impl code to `helpers.rs`, make items `pub(crate)` or `pub` as they were, and add appropriate re-exports in mod.rs.

Important:
- Preserve ALL code exactly
- Fix imports: the moved code uses `use crate::...` which should still work. But sibling modules (non_streaming, streaming) that were using items from mod.rs directly via `super::` will now need `super::helpers::` or the items need to be re-exported from mod.rs
- Use Write tool for new files
- Verify: `cargo check -p moltis-agents 2>&1 | tail -20`

  1000→    pub provider: Option<String>,
  1001→    #[serde(default)]
  1002→    pub citations: Option<String>,
  1003→    #[serde(default)]
  1004→    pub disable_rag: Option<bool>,
  1005→    #[serde(default)]
  1006→    pub llm_reranking: Option<bool>,
  1007→    #[serde(default)]

  1009→    #[serde(default)]
  1010→    pub session_export: Option<String>,
  1011→    #[serde(default)]
  1012→    pub prompt_memory_mode: Option<String>,
  1013→    #[serde(default)]
  1014→    pub qmd_feature_enabled: Option<bool>,
  1015→}
  1016→
  1017→// ── Subscription event types ────────────────────────────────────────────────
  1018→
  1019→#[derive(Debug, SimpleObject, Deserialize, Clone)]
  1020→#[serde(rename_all = "camelCase")]
  1021→pub struct GenericEvent {
  1022→    #[graphql(name = "data")]
  1023→    #[serde(flatten)]
  1024→    pub data: Json,
  1025→}
  1026→
  1027→impl From<serde_json::Value> for GenericEvent {
  1028→    fn from(v: serde_json::Value) -> Self {
  1029→        Self { data: Json(v) }
  1030→    }
  1031→}
  1032→
  1033→/// System heartbeat tick event with timestamp and memory stats.
  1034→#[derive(Debug, SimpleObject, Deserialize, Clone)]
  1035→#[serde(rename_all = "camelCase")]
  1036→pub struct TickEvent {
  1037→    /// Unix timestamp in milliseconds.
  1038→    pub ts: u64,
  1039→    /// Memory usage statistics.
  1040→    pub mem: MemoryStats,
  1041→}
  1042→
  1043→/// Memory usage breakdown.
  1044→#[derive(Debug, SimpleObject, Deserialize, Clone)]
  1045→#[serde(rename_all = "camelCase")]
  1046→pub struct MemoryStats {
  1047→    /// Process RSS in bytes.
  1048→    pub process: u64,
  1049→    /// Approximate bytes held by loaded local llama.cpp model tensors.
  1050→    #[serde(default)]
  1051→    pub local_llama_cpp: u64,
  1052→    /// System available memory in bytes.
  1053→    pub available: u64,
  1054→    /// System total memory in bytes.
  1055→    pub total: u64,
  1056→}
  1057→
  1058→// Allow `Json` to be used as a SimpleObject field (it implements OutputType via Scalar).
  1059→// serde `Deserialize` impl for Json so it can be deserialized from service responses.
  1060→impl<'de> Deserialize<'de> for Json {
  1061→    fn deserialize<D>(deserializer: D) -> Result<Self, D::Error>
  1062→    where
  1063→        D: serde::Deserializer<'de>,
  1064→    {
  1065→        let v = serde_json::Value::deserialize(deserializer)?;
  1066→        Ok(Json(v))
  1067→    }
  1068→}
  1069→
  1070→impl Clone for Json {
  1071→    fn clone(&self) -> Self {
  1072→        Json(self.0.clone())
  1073→    }
  1074→}
  1075→
  1076→impl std::fmt::Debug for Json {
  1077→    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {
  1078→        write!(f, "Json({:?})", self.0)
  1079→    }
  1080→}
  1081→

Move implementation code from `crates/agents/src/runner/mod.rs` (617 lines) into a new sibling file, leaving only module wiring in mod.rs.

Working directory: /Users/penso/.superset/worktrees/moltis/functional-caution

Rule: "Do not add implementation code to `mod.rs` or `lib.rs`. Keep those files for module wiring, exports, and crate setup."

Steps:
1. Read `crates/agents/src/runner/mod.rs` fully (read in parts if needed, ~617 lines)
2. It has submodule declarations: `mod non_streaming; pub mod retry; mod streaming; pub mod tool_result; #[cfg(test)] mod tests; #[cfg(test)] mod tests_legacy;` — keep ALL of these in mod.rs
3. It has `pub use` re-exports — keep those in mod.rs
4. Everything else (imports, constants, types like AgentRunError, AgentRunResult, UsageAccumulator, RunnerEvent, OnEvent, and all ~20 utility functions) goes into a new file
5. Create `crates/agents/src/runner/helpers.rs` with ALL the implementation code (constants, types, functions)
6. Update mod.rs to add `mod helpers;` and re-export what's needed. Since many items are used by the sibling submodules (non_streaming, streaming), check what visibility they need. Items that were `pub(super)` or bare `fn` need to be `pub(super)` in helpers.rs so siblings can still access them via `super::helpers::` or through re-exports.

Actually, the simplest approach: move the impl code to `helpers.rs`, make items `pub(crate)` or `pub` as they were, and add appropriate re-exports in mod.rs.

Important:
- Preserve ALL code exactly
- Fix imports: the moved code uses `use crate::...` which should still work. But sibling modules (non_streaming, streaming) that were using items from mod.rs directly via `super::` will now need `super::helpers::` or the items need to be re-exported from mod.rs
- Use Write tool for new files
- Verify: `cargo check -p moltis-agents 2>&1 | tail -20`

1→//! Compaction strategy dispatcher.
     2→//!
     3→//! Routes a session history through the [`CompactionMode`] selected in
     4→//! `chat.compaction`. Each strategy lives in its own submodule and owns
     5→//! its own tests; shared boundary / pruning / tool-pair helpers live in
     6→//! [`shared`].
     7→//!
     8→//! Submodules:
     9→//! - [`deterministic`] — zero-LLM replace-all extraction.
    10→//! - [`recency_preserving`] — zero-LLM head + middle-marker + tail.
    11→//! - [`structured`] — LLM head + structured-summary + tail (feature-gated).
    12→//! - [`llm_replace`] — LLM streaming-summary replace-all (feature-gated).
    13→//! - [`shared`] — boundary computation, pruning, tool-pair repair,
    14→//!   message builders.
    15→//! - `test_support` — `#[cfg(test)]`-only stub provider for the LLM modes.
    16→//!
    17→//! See `docs/src/compaction.md` for the full mode comparison and trade-off
    18→//! guidance, and the rustdoc on [`moltis_config::CompactionMode`] for
    19→//! per-variant semantics.
    20→
    21→use {
    22→    moltis_agents::model::LlmProvider,
    23→    moltis_config::{CompactionConfig, CompactionMode},
    24→    serde_json::Value,
    25→    thiserror::Error,
    26→};
    27→
    28→mod deterministic;
    29→mod recency_preserving;
    30→mod shared;
    31→
    32→#[cfg(feature = "llm-compaction")]
    33→mod llm_replace;
    34→#[cfg(feature = "llm-compaction")]
    35→mod structured;
    36→
    37→#[cfg(test)]
    38→mod test_support;
    39→
    40→/// Errors surfaced by [`run_compaction`].
    41→///
    42→/// Several variants are gated on the `llm-compaction` cargo feature;
    43→/// when the feature is off the LLM-backed strategies aren't compiled in,
    44→/// so their dedicated error variants become dead code.
    45→#[derive(Debug, Error)]
    46→pub(crate) enum CompactionRunError {
    47→    /// History was empty — nothing to compact.
    48→    #[error("nothing to compact")]
    49→    EmptyHistory,
    50→    /// The strategy produced no summary text.
    51→    #[error("compact produced empty summary")]
    52→    EmptySummary,
    53→    /// A mode that requires an LLM provider was selected but none was passed.
    54→    #[cfg(feature = "llm-compaction")]
    55→    #[error("compaction mode '{mode}' requires an LLM provider to be available for the session")]
    56→    ProviderRequired { mode: &'static str },
    57→    /// The user selected a mode that requires a cargo feature that isn't enabled.
    58→    #[cfg(not(feature = "llm-compaction"))]
    59→    #[error("compaction mode '{mode}' requires the 'llm-compaction' cargo feature to be enabled")]
    60→    FeatureDisabled { mode: &'static str },
    61→    /// The LLM streaming summary call failed.
    62→    #[cfg(feature = "llm-compaction")]
    63→    #[error("compact summarization failed: {0}")]
    64→    LlmFailed(String),
    65→    /// `recency_preserving` couldn't make meaningful progress: the history
    66→    /// is already smaller than `protect_head + protect_tail_min + 1`, and
    67→    /// there was no bulky tool-result content to prune. The caller should
    68→    /// fall back to a different mode (e.g. `deterministic`) rather than
    69→    /// loop.
    70→    #[error(
    71→        "history has {messages} messages — too small for recency_preserving with \
    72→         protect_head={head} and protect_tail_min={tail}; no tool-result pruning \
    73→         was possible either. Try chat.compaction.mode = \"deterministic\" for \
    74→         tiny sessions."
    75→    )]
    76→    TooSmallToCompact {
    77→        messages: usize,
    78→        head: usize,
    79→        tail: usize,
    80→    },
    81→}
    82→
    83→/// Outcome of a successful compaction run.
    84→///
    85→/// Surfaces the effective mode and any token usage so callers can
    86→/// broadcast human-readable metadata to the chat UI ("Compacted via
    87→/// Structured mode, used 1,234 tokens"). `effective_mode` may differ
    88→/// from the user-selected `config.mode` when the `Structured` strategy
    89→/// falls back to `RecencyPreserving` on LLM failure.
    90→#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
    91→pub(crate) struct CompactionOutcome {
    92→    /// Replacement history to write back to the session store.
    93→    pub history: Vec<Value>,
    94→    /// The strategy that actually ran (post-fallback).
    95→    pub effective_mode: CompactionMode,
    96→    /// Input tokens consumed by the summary LLM call, if any. Zero for
    97→    /// the no-LLM strategies and for LLM strategies that fell back.
    98→    pub input_tokens: u32,
    99→    /// Output tokens produced by the summary LLM call, if any. Zero for
   100→    /// the no-LLM strategies and for LLM strategies that fell back.
   101→    pub output_tokens: u32,
   102→}
   103→
   104→impl CompactionOutcome {
   105→    /// Total tokens consumed by the compaction call (sum of input + output).
   106→    #[must_use]
   107→    pub(crate) fn total_tokens(&self) -> u32 {
   108→        self.input_tokens.saturating_add(self.output_tokens)
   109→    }
   110→
   111→    /// Build a JSON metadata fragment suitable for splicing into a
   112→    /// broadcast event payload alongside `state` / `phase`.
   113→    ///
   114→    /// When `include_settings_hint` is true, the [`SETTINGS_HINT`]
   115→    /// text is added under `settingsHint` so UI consumers don't have to
   116→    /// know the wording themselves. Callers pass
   117→    /// `config.show_settings_hint` here to honour the user's preference
   118→    /// for hiding the repetitive footer.
   119→    #[must_use]
   120→    pub(crate) fn broadcast_metadata(&self, include_settings_hint: bool) -> Value {
   121→        let mut fragment = serde_json::json!({
   122→            "mode": compaction_mode_key(self.effective_mode),
   123→            "compactionInputTokens": self.input_tokens,
   124→            "compactionOutputTokens": self.output_tokens,
   125→            "compactionTotalTokens": self.total_tokens(),
   126→        });
   127→        if include_settings_hint && let Some(obj) = fragment.as_object_mut() {
   128→            obj.insert(
   129→                "settingsHint".to_string(),
   130→                Value::String(SETTINGS_HINT.to_string()),
   131→            );
   132→        }
   133→        fragment
   134→    }
   135→}
   136→
   137→/// Short, UI-friendly snake_case name for a compaction mode. Matches the
   138→/// config-file serialisation so consumers can display the exact value a
   139→/// user would paste into `chat.compaction.mode`.
   140→#[must_use]
   141→pub(crate) fn compaction_mode_key(mode: CompactionMode) -> &'static str {
   142→    match mode {
   143→        CompactionMode::Deterministic => "deterministic",
   144→        CompactionMode::RecencyPreserving => "recency_preserving",
   145→        CompactionMode::Structured => "structured",
   146→        CompactionMode::LlmReplace => "llm_replace",
   147→    }
   148→}
   149→
   150→/// Human-readable hint pointing users at the configuration surface so
   151→/// they can change the compaction strategy.
   152→///
   153→/// Kept as a single constant so UI, broadcast events, and any future
   154→/// doc links share the same wording. The reference to
   155→/// `chat.compaction.mode` matches the TOML key so users can paste it
   156→/// straight into `moltis.toml`.
   157→pub(crate) const SETTINGS_HINT: &str = "Change chat.compaction.mode in moltis.toml (or the web UI settings panel) \
   158→     to pick a different compaction strategy. See \
   159→     https://docs.moltis.org/compaction for a comparison of the four modes.";
   160→
   161→/// Best-effort extraction of a human-readable summary body from a
   162→/// compacted history, for use in memory-file snapshots and hook payloads.
   163→///
   164→/// Walks the compacted messages in **reverse** looking for a message whose
   165→/// text content begins with either `[Conversation Summary]` (produced by
   166→/// the `deterministic`, `structured`, and `llm_replace` modes) or
   167→/// `[Conversation Compacted]` (produced by the `recency_preserving`
   168→/// middle marker), and returns the stripped body.
   169→///
   170→/// Reverse iteration matters for iterative `structured` re-compaction:
   171→/// the prior compaction's `[Conversation Summary]` message is preserved
   172→/// verbatim inside the head, and the freshly-generated summary is
   173→/// spliced in at `head_end`. A naïve left-to-right scan would return
   174→/// the stale prior body, so the memory-file snapshot and
   175→/// `AfterCompaction.summary_len` hook payload would describe the old
   176→/// compaction instead of the new one. For the other modes (and for
   177→/// first-time structured) there is only one summary-shaped message, so
   178→/// the reverse walk has no effect.
   179→///
   180→/// Returns an empty string when no summary-shaped message is found,
   181→/// which is fine for hook `summary_len` reporting and falls through
   182→/// gracefully.
   183→#[must_use]
   184→pub(crate) fn extract_summary_body(compacted: &[Value]) -> String {
   185→    compacted
   186→        .iter()
   187→        .rev()
   188→        .filter_map(|msg| msg.get("content").and_then(Value::as_str))
   189→        .find_map(|content| {
   190→            content
   191→                .strip_prefix("[Conversation Summary]\n\n")
   192→                .or_else(|| content.strip_prefix("[Conversation Compacted]\n\n"))
   193→                .map(str::to_string)
   194→        })
   195→        .unwrap_or_default()
   196→}
   197→
   198→/// Run the compaction strategy selected by `config` against `history`.
   199→///
   200→/// Returns a [`CompactionOutcome`] containing the replacement history
   201→/// plus the effective mode and any LLM token usage. Call sites are
   202→/// responsible for writing `outcome.history` back to the session store
   203→/// and for splicing `outcome.broadcast_metadata()` into any
   204→/// `auto_compact` / `compact` broadcast events so users can see what
   205→/// ran and how to change it.
   206→///
   207→/// `provider` is only consulted by LLM-backed modes; pass `None` when
   208→/// no provider has been resolved for the session. LLM modes return
   209→/// [`CompactionRunError::ProviderRequired`] when called without one (or
   210→/// [`CompactionRunError::FeatureDisabled`] when the `llm-compaction`
   211→/// cargo feature is off).
   212→pub(crate) async fn run_compaction(
   213→    history: &[Value],
   214→    config: &CompactionConfig,
   215→    provider: Option<&dyn LlmProvider>,
   216→) -> Result<CompactionOutcome, CompactionRunError> {
   217→    if history.is_empty() {
   218→        return Err(CompactionRunError::EmptyHistory);
   219→    }
   220→
   221→    match config.mode {
   222→        CompactionMode::Deterministic => deterministic::run(history),
   223→        CompactionMode::RecencyPreserving => {
   224→            // `RecencyPreserving` doesn't call the LLM, but it still
   225→            // needs a context window to size the tail-budget token cut.
   226→            // Pull it from the provider if available; otherwise fall
   227→            // back to the 200 K default (matching Claude-3.5-sonnet /
   228→            // the LlmProvider trait default). On small-context models
   229→            // the 200 K fallback would give an oversized tail budget
   230→            // that covers the entire history and causes
   231→            // `TooSmallToCompact`, so log a WARN so operators can see
   232→            // the degraded behaviour in the logs and decide whether to
   233→            // configure a provider or switch to `deterministic` for
   234→            // the session.
   235→            let context_window = if let Some(p) = provider {
   236→                p.context_window()
   237→            } else {
   238→                tracing::warn!(
   239→                    "chat.compact: recency_preserving has no resolved provider, \
   240→                     falling back to a 200 K context-window estimate for the tail \
   241→                     budget. On smaller-context models this may produce an \
   242→                     oversized tail that skips effective compaction — configure \
   243→                     a session provider or set chat.compaction.mode = \"deterministic\""
   244→                );
   245→                200_000
   246→            };
   247→            recency_preserving::run(history, config, context_window)
   248→        },
   249→        CompactionMode::Structured => {
   250→            #[cfg(feature = "llm-compaction")]
   251→            {
   252→                let provider =
   253→                    provider.ok_or(CompactionRunError::ProviderRequired { mode: "structured" })?;
   254→                let context_window = provider.context_window();
   255→                structured::run(history, config, context_window, provider).await
   256→            }
   257→            #[cfg(not(feature = "llm-compaction"))]
   258→            {
   259→                let _ = (config, provider);
   260→                Err(CompactionRunError::FeatureDisabled { mode: "structured" })
   261→            }
   262→        },
   263→        CompactionMode::LlmReplace => {
   264→            #[cfg(feature = "llm-compaction")]
   265→            {
   266→                let provider = provider.ok_or(CompactionRunError::ProviderRequired {
   267→                    mode: "llm_replace",
   268→                })?;
   269→                llm_replace::run(history, config, provider).await
   270→            }
   271→            #[cfg(not(feature = "llm-compaction"))]
   272→            {
   273→                let _ = (config, provider);
   274→                Err(CompactionRunError::FeatureDisabled {
   275→                    mode: "llm_replace",
   276→                })
   277→            }
   278→        },
   279→    }
   280→}
   281→
   282→#[cfg(test)]
   283→#[allow(clippy::unwrap_used, clippy::expect_used)]
   284→mod tests {
   285→    use {super::*, serde_json::json};
   286→
   287→    /// Minimal fixture used by the feature-off tests that exercise the
   288→    /// dispatcher's `FeatureDisabled` branches without going through a
   289→    /// strategy implementation.
   290→    #[cfg(not(feature = "llm-compaction"))]
   291→    fn sample_history() -> Vec<Value> {
   292→        vec![
   293→            json!({"role": "user", "content": "hello"}),
   294→            json!({"role": "assistant", "content": "hi there"}),
   295→        ]
   296→    }
   297→
   298→    // ── Dispatcher ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   299→
   300→    #[tokio::test]
   301→    async fn empty_history_returns_empty_history_error() {
   302→        let config = CompactionConfig::default();
   303→        let err = run_compaction(&[], &config, None).await.unwrap_err();
   304→        assert!(matches!(err, CompactionRunError::EmptyHistory));
   305→    }
   306→
   307→    #[cfg(not(feature = "llm-compaction"))]
   308→    #[tokio::test]
   309→    async fn llm_replace_mode_returns_feature_disabled_when_feature_off() {
   310→        let config = CompactionConfig {
   311→            mode: CompactionMode::LlmReplace,
   312→            ..Default::default()
   313→        };
   314→        let err = run_compaction(&sample_history(), &config, None)
   315→            .await
   316→            .unwrap_err();
   317→        match err {
   318→            CompactionRunError::FeatureDisabled { mode } => assert_eq!(mode, "llm_replace"),
   319→            other => panic!("expected FeatureDisabled, got {other:?}"),
   320→        }
   321→    }
   322→
   323→    #[cfg(not(feature = "llm-compaction"))]
   324→    #[tokio::test]
   325→    async fn structured_mode_returns_feature_disabled_when_feature_off() {
   326→        let config = CompactionConfig {
   327→            mode: CompactionMode::Structured,
   328→            ..Default::default()
   329→        };
   330→        let err = run_compaction(&sample_history(), &config, None)
   331→            .await
   332→            .unwrap_err();
   333→        match err {
   334→            CompactionRunError::FeatureDisabled { mode } => assert_eq!(mode, "structured"),
   335→            other => panic!("expected FeatureDisabled, got {other:?}"),
   336→        }
   337→    }
   338→
   339→    // ── CompactionOutcome broadcast metadata ──────────────────────────
   340→
   341→    #[test]
   342→    fn broadcast_metadata_exposes_mode_and_tokens() {
   343→        let outcome = CompactionOutcome {
   344→            history: vec![json!({"role": "user", "content": "ok"})],
   345→            effective_mode: CompactionMode::Structured,
   346→            input_tokens: 1_234,
   347→            output_tokens: 567,
   348→        };
   349→        let meta = outcome.broadcast_metadata(true);
   350→        assert_eq!(meta["mode"], "structured");
   351→        assert_eq!(meta["compactionInputTokens"], 1_234);
   352→        assert_eq!(meta["compactionOutputTokens"], 567);
   353→        assert_eq!(meta["compactionTotalTokens"], 1_801);
   354→        assert!(
   355→            !meta["settingsHint"]
   356→                .as_str()
   357→                .expect("settingsHint is string")
   358→                .is_empty()
   359→        );
   360→    }
   361→
   362→    #[test]
   363→    fn broadcast_metadata_omits_settings_hint_when_disabled() {
   364→        let outcome = CompactionOutcome {
   365→            history: vec![json!({"role": "user", "content": "ok"})],
   366→            effective_mode: CompactionMode::Deterministic,
   367→            input_tokens: 0,
   368→            output_tokens: 0,
   369→        };
   370→        let meta = outcome.broadcast_metadata(false);
   371→        assert_eq!(meta["mode"], "deterministic");
   372→        assert_eq!(meta["compactionTotalTokens"], 0);
   373→        assert!(
   374→            meta.get("settingsHint").is_none(),
   375→            "settingsHint must be absent when include_settings_hint=false, got: {meta}"
   376→        );
   377→    }
   378→
   379→    #[test]
   380→    fn compaction_mode_key_matches_toml_serialization() {
   381→        // The keys must match exactly what users would paste into
   382→        // `chat.compaction.mode` in moltis.toml, so UI consumers can
   383→        // display them and offer a "copy to clipboard" action.
   384→        assert_eq!(
   385→            compaction_mode_key(CompactionMode::Deterministic),
   386→            "deterministic"
   387→        );
   388→        assert_eq!(
   389→            compaction_mode_key(CompactionMode::RecencyPreserving),
   390→            "recency_preserving"
   391→        );
   392→        assert_eq!(
   393→            compaction_mode_key(CompactionMode::Structured),
   394→            "structured"
   395→        );
   396→        assert_eq!(
   397→            compaction_mode_key(CompactionMode::LlmReplace),
   398→            "llm_replace"
   399→        );
   400→    }
   401→
   402→    // ── extract_summary_body (memory-file / hook helper) ──────────────
   403→
   404→    #[test]
   405→    fn extract_summary_body_finds_conversation_summary_prefix() {
   406→        // deterministic / structured / llm_replace shape: single summary
   407→        // message at index 0.
   408→        let compacted = vec![json!({
   409→            "role": "user",
   410→            "content": "[Conversation Summary]\n\nBody text here",
   411→        })];
   412→        assert_eq!(extract_summary_body(&compacted), "Body text here");
   413→    }
   414→
   415→    #[test]
   416→    fn extract_summary_body_finds_conversation_compacted_marker() {
   417→        // recency_preserving shape: head verbatim, then middle marker,
   418→        // then tail verbatim. The marker is NOT at index 0.
   419→        let compacted = vec![
   420→            json!({"role": "user", "content": "first user"}),
   421→            json!({"role": "assistant", "content": "first reply"}),
   422→            json!({
   423→                "role": "user",
   424→                "content": "[Conversation Compacted]\n\n6 earlier messages were elided …",
   425→            }),
   426→            json!({"role": "user", "content": "recent user"}),
   427→            json!({"role": "assistant", "content": "recent reply"}),
   428→        ];
   429→        let body = extract_summary_body(&compacted);
   430→        assert!(
   431→            body.starts_with("6 earlier messages were elided"),
   432→            "got: {body}"
   433→        );
   434→    }
   435→
   436→    #[test]
   437→    fn extract_summary_body_returns_empty_when_no_summary_shaped_message_present() {
   438→        // Pathological: history with no summary-shaped message. Helper
   439→        // should return "" rather than picking up unrelated content.
   440→        let compacted = vec![
   441→            json!({"role": "user", "content": "just a regular user turn"}),
   442→            json!({"role": "assistant", "content": "just a regular reply"}),
   443→        ];
   444→        assert_eq!(extract_summary_body(&compacted), "");
   445→    }
   446→
   447→    #[test]
   448→    fn extract_summary_body_picks_newest_summary_in_iterative_structured_recompaction() {
   449→        // Iterative `structured` re-compaction shape: the prior
   450→        // compaction's `[Conversation Summary]` sits in the retained
   451→        // head at index 0, and the freshly-generated summary is spliced
   452→        // in at `head_end`. The memory-file snapshot and
   453→        // AfterCompaction hook need the NEWEST summary body, not the
   454→        // stale one.
   455→        let compacted = vec![
   456→            json!({
   457→                "role": "user",
   458→                "content": "[Conversation Summary]\n\nOld summary body from a previous compaction",
   459→            }),
   460→            json!({"role": "assistant", "content": "ok got it"}),
   461→            json!({
   462→                "role": "user",
   463→                "content": "[Conversation Summary]\n\nFresh summary body from this compaction",
   464→            }),
   465→            json!({"role": "user", "content": "recent user turn"}),
   466→            json!({"role": "assistant", "content": "recent reply"}),
   467→        ];
   468→        assert_eq!(
   469→            extract_summary_body(&compacted),
   470→            "Fresh summary body from this compaction"
   471→        );
   472→    }
   473→}

   276→fn is_in(method: &str, list: &[&str]) -> bool {
   277→    list.contains(&method)
   278→}
   279→
   280→/// Check role + scopes for a method. Returns None if authorized, Some(error) if not.
   281→pub fn authorize_method(method: &str, role: &str, scopes: &[String]) -> Option<ErrorShape> {
   282→    use moltis_protocol::scopes as s;
   283→
   284→    if is_in(method, NODE_METHODS) {
   285→        if role == "node" {
   286→            return None;
   287→        }
   288→        return Some(ErrorShape::new(
   289→            error_codes::FORBIDDEN,
   290→            format!("unauthorized role: {role}"),
   291→        ));
   292→    }
   293→    if role == "node" || role != "operator" {
   294→        return Some(ErrorShape::new(
   295→            error_codes::FORBIDDEN,
   296→            format!("unauthorized role: {role}"),
   297→        ));
   298→    }
   299→
   300→    let has = |scope: &str| scopes.iter().any(|s| s == scope);
   301→    if has(s::ADMIN) {
   302→        return None;
   303→    }
   304→
   305→    if is_in(method, APPROVAL_METHODS) && !has(s::APPROVALS) {
   306→        return Some(ErrorShape::new(
   307→            error_codes::UNAUTHORIZED,
   308→            "missing scope: operator.approvals",
   309→        ));
   310→    }
   311→    if is_in(method, PAIRING_METHODS) && !has(s::PAIRING) {
   312→        return Some(ErrorShape::new(
   313→            error_codes::UNAUTHORIZED,
   314→            "missing scope: operator.pairing",
   315→        ));
   316→    }
   317→    if is_in(method, READ_METHODS) && !(has(s::READ) || has(s::WRITE)) {
   318→        return Some(ErrorShape::new(
   319→            error_codes::UNAUTHORIZED,
   320→            "missing scope: operator.read",
   321→        ));
   322→    }
   323→    if is_in(method, WRITE_METHODS) && !has(s::WRITE) {
   324→        return Some(ErrorShape::new(
   325→            error_codes::UNAUTHORIZED,
   326→            "missing scope: operator.write",
   327→        ));
   328→    }
   329→
   330→    if is_in(method, APPROVAL_METHODS)
   331→        || is_in(method, PAIRING_METHODS)
   332→        || is_in(method, READ_METHODS)
   333→        || is_in(method, WRITE_METHODS)
   334→    {
   335→        return None;
   336→    }
   337→
   338→    Some(ErrorShape::new(
   339→        error_codes::UNAUTHORIZED,
   340→        "missing scope: operator.admin",
   341→    ))
   342→}
   343→
   344→// ── Method registry ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   345→
   346→pub struct MethodRegistry {
   347→    handlers: HashMap<String, HandlerFn>,
   348→}
   349→
   350→impl Default for MethodRegistry {
   351→    fn default() -> Self {
   352→        Self::new()
   353→    }
   354→}
   355→
   356→impl MethodRegistry {
   357→    pub fn new() -> Self {
   358→        let mut reg = Self {
   359→            handlers: HashMap::new(),
   360→        };
   361→        reg.register_defaults();
   362→        reg
   363→    }
   364→
   365→    pub fn register(&mut self, method: impl Into<String>, handler: HandlerFn) {
   366→        self.handlers.insert(method.into(), handler);
   367→    }
   368→
   369→    pub async fn dispatch(&self, ctx: MethodContext) -> ResponseFrame {
   370→        let method = ctx.method.clone();
   371→        let request_id = ctx.request_id.clone();
   372→        let conn_id = ctx.client_conn_id.clone();
   373→
   374→        if let Some(err) = authorize_method(&method, &ctx.client_role, &ctx.client_scopes) {
   375→            warn!(method, conn_id = %conn_id, code = %err.code, "method auth denied");
   376→            return ResponseFrame::err(&request_id, err);
   377→        }
   378→
   379→        let Some(handler) = self.handlers.get(&method) else {
   380→            warn!(method, conn_id = %conn_id, "unknown method");
   381→            return ResponseFrame::err(
   382→                &request_id,
   383→                ErrorShape::new(
   384→                    error_codes::UNKNOWN_METHOD,
   385→                    format!("unknown method: {method}"),
   386→                ),
   387→            );
   388→        };
   389→
   390→        debug!(method, request_id = %request_id, conn_id = %conn_id, "dispatching method");
   391→        match handler(ctx).await {
   392→            Ok(payload) => {
   393→                debug!(method, request_id = %request_id, "method ok");
   394→                ResponseFrame::ok(&request_id, payload)
   395→            },
   396→            Err(err) => {
   397→                if err.code == error_codes::UNAVAILABLE {
   398→                    debug!(method, request_id = %request_id, code = %err.code, msg = %err.message, "method unavailable");
   399→                } else {
   400→                    warn!(method, request_id = %request_id, code = %err.code, msg = %err.message, "method error");
   401→                }
   402→                ResponseFrame::err(&request_id, err)
   403→            },
   404→        }
   405→    }
   406→
   407→    pub fn method_names(&self) -> Vec<String> {
   408→        let mut names: Vec<_> = self.handlers.keys().cloned().collect();
   409→        names.sort();
   410→        names
   411→    }
   412→
   413→    fn register_defaults(&mut self) {
   414→        gateway::register(self);
   415→        node::register(self);
   416→        pairing::register(self);
   417→        services::register(self);
   418→        subscribe::register(self);
   419→        channel_mux::register(self);
   420→    }
   421→}
   422→
   423→/// Load the disabled hooks set from `data_dir/disabled_hooks.json`.
   424→pub(crate) fn load_disabled_hooks() -> std::collections::HashSet<String> {
   425→    let path = moltis_config::data_dir().join("disabled_hooks.json");
   426→    std::fs::read_to_string(&path)
   427→        .ok()
   428→        .and_then(|s| serde_json::from_str(&s).ok())
   429→        .unwrap_or_default()
   430→}
   431→
   432→#[cfg(test)]
   433→#[allow(clippy::unwrap_used, clippy::expect_used)]
   434→mod tests {
   435→    use super::*;
   436→
   437→    fn scopes(s: &[&str]) -> Vec<String> {
   438→        s.iter().map(|x| x.to_string()).collect()
   439→    }
   440→
   441→    fn assert_error_code(result: Option<ErrorShape>, expected_code: &str) {
   442→        let err = result.expect("expected an error");
   443→        assert_eq!(err.code, expected_code, "wrong error code: {}", err.message);
   444→    }
   445→
   446→    #[test]
   447→    fn senders_list_requires_read() {
   448→        assert!(
   449→            authorize_method(
   450→                "channels.senders.list",
   451→                "operator",
   452→                &scopes(&["operator.read"])
   453→            )
   454→            .is_none()
   455→        );
   456→        assert_error_code(
   457→            authorize_method("channels.senders.list", "operator", &scopes(&[])),
   458→            "UNAUTHORIZED",
   459→        );
   460→    }
   461→
   462→    #[test]
   463→    fn senders_approve_requires_write() {
