1→use std::sync::Arc;
     2→
     3→use moltis_channels::{ChannelReplyTarget, Error as ChannelError, Result as ChannelResult};
     4→
     5→use crate::state::GatewayState;
     6→
     7→use super::resolve_channel_session;
     8→
     9→mod attachments;
    10→mod control_handlers;
    11→pub(in crate::channel_events) mod formatting;
    12→mod location;
    13→mod media;
    14→mod session_handlers;
    15→
    16→// Re-export everything that `channel_events.rs` uses via `commands::*`.
    17→pub(super) use {
    18→    attachments::dispatch_to_chat_with_attachments,
    19→    location::{resolve_pending_location, update_location},
    20→    media::{
    21→        request_sender_approval, save_channel_attachment, save_channel_voice, transcribe_voice,
    22→        voice_stt_available,
    23→    },
    24→};
    25→
    26→pub(super) async fn dispatch_interaction(
    27→    state: &Arc<tokio::sync::OnceCell<Arc<GatewayState>>>,
    28→    callback_data: &str,
    29→    reply_to: ChannelReplyTarget,
    30→) -> ChannelResult<String> {
    31→    // Map callback_data prefixes to slash-command text, following the same
    32→    // convention used by Telegram's handle_callback_query.
    33→    let cmd_text = if let Some(n) = callback_data.strip_prefix("sessions_switch:") {
    34→        format!("sessions {n}")
    35→    } else if let Some(n) = callback_data.strip_prefix("agent_switch:") {
    36→        format!("agent {n}")
    37→    } else if let Some(n) = callback_data.strip_prefix("model_switch:") {
    38→        format!("model {n}")
    39→    } else if let Some(val) = callback_data.strip_prefix("sandbox_toggle:") {
    40→        format!("sandbox {val}")
    41→    } else if let Some(n) = callback_data.strip_prefix("sandbox_image:") {
    42→        format!("sandbox image {n}")
    43→    } else if let Some(provider) = callback_data.strip_prefix("model_provider:") {
    44→        format!("model provider:{provider}")
    45→    } else {
    46→        return Err(ChannelError::invalid_input(format!(
    47→            "unknown interaction callback: {callback_data}"
    48→        )));
    49→    };
    50→
    51→    dispatch_command(state, &cmd_text, reply_to, None).await
    52→}
    53→
    54→pub(super) async fn dispatch_command(
    55→    state: &Arc<tokio::sync::OnceCell<Arc<GatewayState>>>,
    56→    command: &str,
    57→    reply_to: ChannelReplyTarget,
    58→    sender_id: Option<&str>,
    59→) -> ChannelResult<String> {
    60→    let state = state
    61→        .get()
    62→        .ok_or_else(|| ChannelError::unavailable("gateway not ready"))?;
    63→    let session_metadata = state
    64→        .services
    65→        .session_metadata
    66→        .as_ref()
    67→        .ok_or_else(|| ChannelError::unavailable("session metadata not available"))?;
    68→    let session_key = resolve_channel_session(&reply_to, session_metadata).await;
    69→
    70→    // Extract the command name (first word) and args (rest).
    71→    let cmd = command.split_whitespace().next().unwrap_or("");
    72→    let args = command[cmd.len()..].trim();
    73→
    74→    match cmd {
    75→        // Session management commands
    76→        "new" => {
    77→            session_handlers::handle_new(
    78→                state,
    79→                session_metadata,
    80→                &session_key,
    81→                &reply_to,
    82→                sender_id,
    83→            )
    84→            .await
    85→        },
    86→        "clear" => session_handlers::handle_clear(state, &session_key).await,
    87→        "compact" => session_handlers::handle_compact(state, &session_key).await,
    88→        "context" => session_handlers::handle_context(state, &session_key).await,
    89→        "sessions" => {
    90→            session_handlers::handle_sessions(
    91→                state,
    92→                session_metadata,
    93→                &session_key,
    94→                &reply_to,
    95→                args,
    96→            )
    97→            .await
    98→        },
    99→        "attach" => {
   100→            session_handlers::handle_attach(state, session_metadata, &session_key, &reply_to, args)
   101→                .await
   102→        },
   103→
   104→        // Control commands
   105→        "approvals" => control_handlers::handle_approvals(state, &session_key).await,
   106→        "approve" | "deny" => {
   107→            control_handlers::handle_approve_deny(
   108→                state,
   109→                &session_key,
   110→                &reply_to,
   111→                sender_id,
   112→                cmd,
   113→                args,
   114→            )
   115→            .await
   116→        },
   117→        "agent" => {
   118→            control_handlers::handle_agent(state, session_metadata, &session_key, args).await
   119→        },
   120→        "model" => {
   121→            control_handlers::handle_model(state, session_metadata, &session_key, args).await
   122→        },
   123→        "sandbox" => {
   124→            control_handlers::handle_sandbox(state, session_metadata, &session_key, args).await
   125→        },
   126→        "sh" => control_handlers::handle_sh(state, &session_key, args).await,
   127→        "stop" => control_handlers::handle_stop(state, &session_key).await,
   128→        "peek" => control_handlers::handle_peek(state, &session_key).await,
   129→        _ => Err(ChannelError::invalid_input(format!(
   130→            "unknown command: /{cmd}"
   131→        ))),
   132→    }
   133→}
   134→

1→//! HTTP server entry points, middleware stack, and router construction.
     2→//!
     3→//! This module contains the HTTP-specific layer of the moltis gateway:
     4→//! `AppState`, router building, middleware, handlers, and server startup.
     5→//! Core business logic lives in `moltis-gateway`; this crate depends on it
     6→//! but never the reverse.
     7→
     8→use std::{net::SocketAddr, path::PathBuf, sync::Arc};

    36→    broadcast::{BroadcastOpts, broadcast, broadcast_tick},

    41→

    46→
    47→#[cfg(feature = "tls")]
    48→use moltis_tls::CertManager;
    49→
    50→// ── Submodules ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    51→
    52→mod builder;
    53→mod gateway;
    54→mod handlers;
    55→mod middleware;
    56→mod ngrok;
    57→mod runtime;
    58→
    59→// ── Re-exports ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    60→//
    61→// Keep the public API surface identical to the original single-file module.
    62→
    63→pub use {
    64→    builder::{build_gateway_app, build_gateway_base, finalize_gateway_app},
    65→    gateway::prepare_gateway,
    66→    runtime::{prepare_httpd_embedded, start_gateway},

    68→
    69→#[cfg(feature = "ngrok")]

    73→
    74→pub use handlers::is_same_origin;
    75→
    76→// Bring submodule internals into scope so that `handlers.rs` and `runtime.rs`
    77→// (which use `use super::*;`) can access peer-module items via glob import.
    78→//
    79→// `handlers::*` — runtime.rs relies on `resolve_outbound_ip`, `tls_runtime_sans`,
    80→//   `startup_bind_line`, `startup_passkey_origin_lines`, `startup_setup_code_lines`.
    81→// `builder::build_gateway_base_internal` — handlers.rs tests (ngrok-only).
    82→// `runtime::{attach_ngrok_controller_owner, ngrok_loopback_has_proxy_headers}` —
    83→//   handlers.rs tests (ngrok-only).
    84→#[cfg(feature = "ngrok")]
    85→#[cfg(test)]
    86→use builder::build_gateway_base_internal;
    87→#[allow(unused_imports)]
    88→use handlers::*;
    89→#[cfg(feature = "ngrok")]
    90→#[cfg(test)]
    91→use runtime::{attach_ngrok_controller_owner, ngrok_loopback_has_proxy_headers};
    92→
    93→// ── Shared app state ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    94→
    95→/// Options for tailscale serve/funnel passed from CLI flags.
    96→#[cfg(feature = "tailscale")]
    97→pub struct TailscaleOpts {
    98→    pub mode: String,
    99→    pub reset_on_exit: bool,
   100→}
   101→
   102→#[derive(Clone)]
   103→pub struct AppState {
   104→    pub gateway: Arc<GatewayState>,
   105→    pub methods: Arc<MethodRegistry>,
   106→    pub request_throttle: Arc<crate::request_throttle::RequestThrottle>,
   107→    pub webauthn_registry: Option<SharedWebAuthnRegistry>,
   108→    #[cfg(feature = "ngrok")]

   116→    #[cfg(feature = "graphql")]
   117→    pub graphql_schema: moltis_graphql::MoltisSchema,
   118→}
   119→
   120→/// Function signature for adding extra routes (e.g. web-UI) to the gateway.
   121→pub type RouteEnhancer = fn() -> Router<AppState>;
   122→
   123→#[cfg(feature = "ngrok")]
   124→type GatewayBase = (Router<AppState>, AppState, Arc<NgrokController>);
   125→
   126→#[cfg(not(feature = "ngrok"))]
   127→type GatewayBase = (Router<AppState>, AppState);
   128→
   129→// ── Prepared gateway types ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
   130→
   131→/// A fully wired gateway (app router + shared state), ready to be served.
   132→///
   133→/// Created by [`prepare_gateway`]. Callers bind their own TCP listener and
   134→/// feed `app` to `axum::serve` (or an equivalent). Background tasks (metrics,
   135→/// MCP health, cron, etc.) are already spawned on the current tokio runtime.
   136→pub struct PreparedGateway {
   137→    /// The composed application router.
   138→    pub app: Router,
   139→    /// Shared gateway state (sessions, services, config, etc.).
   140→    pub state: Arc<GatewayState>,
   141→    /// The port the gateway was configured for.
   142→    pub port: u16,
   143→    /// Metadata collected during setup, used by [`start_gateway`] for the
   144→    /// startup banner. Not relevant for bridge callers.
   145→    pub(crate) banner: BannerMeta,
   146→    /// Network audit buffer for real-time streaming (present when
   147→    /// the `trusted-network` feature is enabled and the proxy is active).
   148→    #[cfg(feature = "trusted-network")]
   149→    pub audit_buffer: Option<moltis_gateway::network_audit::NetworkAuditBuffer>,
   150→    /// Keeps the trusted-network proxy alive for the server's full lifetime.
   151→    /// Dropping this sender closes the watch channel, which is the proxy's
   152→    /// shutdown signal.
   153→    #[cfg(feature = "trusted-network")]
   154→    pub _proxy_shutdown_tx: Option<tokio::sync::watch::Sender<bool>>,
   155→}
   156→
   157→/// Internal metadata for the startup banner printed by [`start_gateway`].
   158→pub struct BannerMeta {
   159→    pub provider_summary: String,
   160→    pub mcp_configured_count: usize,
   161→    pub method_count: usize,
   162→    pub sandbox_backend_name: String,
   163→    pub data_dir: PathBuf,
   164→    pub openclaw_status: String,
   165→    pub setup_code_display: Option<String>,
   166→    pub webauthn_registry: Option<SharedWebAuthnRegistry>,
   167→    #[cfg(feature = "ngrok")]
   168→    pub ngrok_controller: Arc<NgrokController>,
   169→    pub browser_for_lifecycle: Arc<dyn moltis_gateway::services::BrowserService>,
   170→    pub browser_tool_for_warmup: Option<Arc<dyn moltis_agents::tool_registry::AgentTool>>,
   171→    pub config: moltis_config::schema::MoltisConfig,
   172→    #[cfg(feature = "tailscale")]
   173→    pub tailscale_mode: TailscaleMode,
   174→    #[cfg(feature = "tailscale")]
   175→    pub tailscale_reset_on_exit: bool,
   176→}

//! GraphQL output and input types.
//!
//! These types are deserialized from the JSON values returned by service
//! methods. They use `#[derive(SimpleObject)]` for output types and
//! `#[derive(InputObject)]` for input types. Fields use `serde` for
//! deserialization and `async-graphql` for schema generation.
//!
//! For dynamic/untyped fields, the `Json` scalar is used.

use {async_graphql::SimpleObject, serde::Deserialize};

use crate::scalars::Json;

// ── Common result type ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────

/// Generic boolean result for mutations that return `{ "ok": true }`.
#[derive(Debug, SimpleObject, Deserialize)]
pub struct BoolResult {
    pub ok: bool,
}

// ── Health & Status ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

#[derive(Debug, SimpleObject, Deserialize)]
#[serde(rename_all = "camelCase")]
pub struct HealthInfo {
    pub ok: bool,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub connections: Option<u64>,
}

#[derive(Debug, SimpleObject, Deserialize)]
#[serde(rename_all = "camelCase")]
pub struct StatusInfo {
    #[serde(default)]
    pub hostname: Option<String>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub version: Option<String>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub connections: Option<u64>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub uptime_ms: Option<u64>,
}

// ── System Presence ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

#[derive(Debug, SimpleObject, Deserialize)]
#[serde(rename_all = "camelCase")]
pub struct SystemPresence {
    #[serde(default)]
    pub clients: Vec<ClientInfo>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub nodes: Vec<NodeInfo>,
}

#[derive(Debug, SimpleObject, Deserialize)]
#[serde(rename_all = "camelCase")]
pub struct ClientInfo {
    #[serde(default)]
    pub conn_id: Option<String>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub role: Option<String>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub connected_at: Option<u64>,
}

#[derive(Debug, SimpleObject, Deserialize)]
#[serde(rename_all = "camelCase")]
pub struct NodeInfo {
    #[serde(default)]
    pub node_id: Option<String>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub conn_id: Option<String>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub display_name: Option<String>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub platform: Option<String>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub version: Option<String>,
}

// ── Sessions ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

#[derive(Debug, SimpleObject, Deserialize)]
#[serde(rename_all = "camelCase")]
pub struct SessionEntry {
    #[serde(default)]
    pub id: Option<String>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub key: Option<String>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub label: Option<String>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub model: Option<String>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub created_at: Option<u64>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub updated_at: Option<u64>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub message_count: Option<u64>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub last_seen_message_count: Option<u64>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub project_id: Option<String>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub archived: Option<bool>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub worktree_branch: Option<String>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub sandbox_enabled: Option<bool>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub sandbox_image: Option<String>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub channel_binding: Option<String>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub parent_session_key: Option<String>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub fork_point: Option<u64>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub preview: Option<String>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub mcp_disabled: Option<bool>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub replying: Option<bool>,
}

/// Whether a session currently has an active LLM run (waiting for response).
#[derive(Debug, SimpleObject, Deserialize)]
#[serde(rename_all = "camelCase")]
pub struct SessionActiveResult {
    pub active: bool,
}

#[derive(Debug, SimpleObject, Deserialize)]
#[serde(rename_all = "camelCase")]
pub struct SessionBranch {
    #[serde(default)]
    pub key: Option<String>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub label: Option<String>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub fork_point: Option<u64>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub message_count: Option<u64>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub created_at: Option<u64>,
}

#[derive(Debug, SimpleObject, Deserialize)]
#[serde(rename_all = "camelCase")]
pub struct SessionShareResult {
    #[serde(default)]
    pub id: Option<String>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub session_key: Option<String>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub visibility: Option<String>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub path: Option<String>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub views: Option<u64>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub created_at: Option<u64>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub revoked_at: Option<u64>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub snapshot_message_count: Option<u64>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub access_key: Option<String>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub notice: Option<String>,
}

// ── Chat ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

#[derive(Debug, SimpleObject, Deserialize)]
#[serde(rename_all = "camelCase")]
pub struct ChatRawPrompt {
    #[serde(default)]
    pub prompt: Option<String>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub char_count: Option<u64>,
    #[serde(default, alias = "native_tools")]
    pub native_tools: Option<bool>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub tool_count: Option<u64>,
}

// ── Cron ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

#[derive(Debug, SimpleObject, Deserialize)]
#[serde(rename_all = "camelCase")]
pub struct CronJob {
    #[serde(default)]
    pub id: Option<String>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub name: Option<String>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub enabled: Option<bool>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub delete_after_run: Option<bool>,
    #[graphql(name = "schedule")]
    #[serde(default)]
    pub schedule: Option<Json>,
    #[graphql(name = "payload")]
    #[serde(default)]
    pub payload: Option<Json>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub session_target: Option<String>,
    #[graphql(name = "state")]
    #[serde(default)]
    pub state: Option<Json>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub created_at_ms: Option<u64>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub updated_at_ms: Option<u64>,
}

#[derive(Debug, SimpleObject, Deserialize)]
#[serde(rename_all = "camelCase")]
pub struct CronStatus {
    #[serde(default)]
    pub running: Option<bool>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub job_count: Option<u64>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub enabled_count: Option<u64>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub next_run_at_ms: Option<u64>,
}

#[derive(Debug, SimpleObject, Deserialize)]
#[serde(rename_all = "camelCase")]
pub struct CronRunRecord {
    #[serde(default)]
    pub job_id: Option<String>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub started_at_ms: Option<u64>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub finished_at_ms: Option<u64>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub status: Option<String>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub error: Option<String>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub duration_ms: Option<u64>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub output: Option<String>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub input_tokens: Option<u64>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub output_tokens: Option<u64>,
}

#[derive(Debug, SimpleObject, Deserialize)]
#[serde(rename_all = "camelCase")]
pub struct HeartbeatStatus {
    #[serde(default)]
    pub config: Option<HeartbeatConfig>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub job: Option<CronJob>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub prompt_source: Option<String>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub heartbeat_file_exists: Option<bool>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub has_prompt: Option<bool>,
}

#[derive(Debug, SimpleObject, Deserialize)]
pub struct HeartbeatConfig {
    #[serde(default)]
    pub enabled: Option<bool>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub every: Option<String>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub model: Option<String>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub prompt: Option<String>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub ack_max_chars: Option<u64>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub active_hours: Option<HeartbeatActiveHours>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub deliver: Option<bool>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub channel: Option<String>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub to: Option<String>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub sandbox_enabled: Option<bool>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub sandbox_image: Option<String>,
}

#[derive(Debug, SimpleObject, Deserialize)]
pub struct HeartbeatActiveHours {
    #[serde(default)]
    pub start: Option<String>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub end: Option<String>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub timezone: Option<String>,
}

// ── Projects ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

#[derive(Debug, SimpleObject, Deserialize)]
#[serde(rename_all = "camelCase")]
pub struct Project {
    #[serde(default)]
    pub id: Option<String>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub label: Option<String>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub directory: Option<String>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub system_prompt: Option<String>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub auto_worktree: Option<bool>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub setup_command: Option<String>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub teardown_command: Option<String>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub branch_prefix: Option<String>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub sandbox_image: Option<String>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub detected: Option<bool>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub created_at: Option<String>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub updated_at: Option<String>,
}

#[derive(Debug, SimpleObject, Deserialize)]
#[serde(rename_all = "camelCase")]
pub struct ProjectContext {
    #[serde(default)]
    pub project: Option<Project>,
    #[serde(default, alias = "context_files")]
    pub context_files: Option<Vec<ContextFile>>,
}

#[derive(Debug, SimpleObject, Deserialize)]
#[serde(rename_all = "camelCase")]
pub struct ContextFile {
    #[serde(default)]
    pub path: Option<String>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub content: Option<String>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub kind: Option<String>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub warnings: Option<Vec<ContextWarning>>,
}

#[derive(Debug, SimpleObject, Deserialize)]
#[serde(rename_all = "camelCase")]
pub struct ContextWarning {
    #[serde(default)]
    pub code: Option<String>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub severity: Option<String>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub message: Option<String>,
}

// ── Channels ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

#[derive(Debug, SimpleObject, Deserialize)]
#[serde(rename_all = "camelCase")]
pub struct ChannelInfo {
    #[serde(default)]
    pub name: Option<String>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub r#type: Option<String>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub status: Option<String>,
    #[serde(default)]
    pub account_id: Option<String>,
}

#[derive(Debug, SimpleObject, Deserialize)]
#[serde(rename_all = "camelCase")]
pub struct ChannelSender {

// Allow `Json` to be used as a SimpleObject field (it implements OutputType via Scalar).
// serde `Deserialize` impl for Json so it can be deserialized from service responses.
impl<'de> Deserialize<'de> for Json {
    fn deserialize<D>(deserializer: D) -> Result<Self, D::Error>
    where
        D: serde::Deserializer<'de>,
    {
        let v = serde_json::Value::deserialize(deserializer)?;
        Ok(Json(v))
    }
}

impl Clone for Json {
    fn clone(&self) -> Self {
        Json(self.0.clone())
    }
}

impl std::fmt::Debug for Json {
    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {
        write!(f, "Json({:?})", self.0)
    }
}

content.text:
The refactoring is complete. Here is what was done:

**Summary:**

- **`crates/agents/src/runner/mod.rs`** (617 lines -> 42 lines): Now contains only module declarations, `pub use` re-exports for the public API, and `pub(crate) use` re-exports so sibling submodules (`non_streaming`, `streaming`, test modules) can continue importing via `super::`. No implementation code remains.

- **`crates/agents/src/runner/helpers.rs`** (new, 597 lines): Contains all the implementation code that was previously in `mod.rs`:
  - Constants (`AUTO_CONTINUE_NUDGE`, `MALFORMED_TOOL_RETRY_PROMPT`, compaction thresholds, etc.)
  - Types (`AgentRunError`, `AgentRunResult`, `UsageAccumulator`, `OnEvent`, `RunnerEvent`)
  - All ~20 utility functions (`sanitize_tool_name`, `dispatch_after_llm_call_hook`, `finish_agent_run`, `enforce_tool_result_context_budget`, `apply_loop_detector_intervention`, etc.)

**Key decisions:**
- Items used by production sibling modules are re-exported unconditionally as `pub(crate)` from `mod.rs`
- Items only used by test submodules are gated with `#[cfg(test)]` to avoid unused-import warnings
- All items in `helpers.rs` use `pub(crate)` visibility (or `pub` for the public API types) so they can be re-exported from `mod.rs`
- No changes were needed in `non_streaming.rs`, `streaming.rs`, or test modules since they all import via `super::` which still resolves correctly through the re-exports

**Verification:** `cargo check` passes cleanly (no warnings), and all 345 tests in `moltis-agents` pass.

1→//! Compaction strategy dispatcher.
     2→//!
     3→//! Routes a session history through the [`CompactionMode`] selected in
     4→//! `chat.compaction`. Each strategy lives in its own submodule and owns
     5→//! its own tests; shared boundary / pruning / tool-pair helpers live in
     6→//! [`shared`].
     7→//!
     8→//! Submodules:
     9→//! - [`deterministic`] — zero-LLM replace-all extraction.
    10→//! - [`recency_preserving`] — zero-LLM head + middle-marker + tail.
    11→//! - [`structured`] — LLM head + structured-summary + tail (feature-gated).
    12→//! - [`llm_replace`] — LLM streaming-summary replace-all (feature-gated).
